titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Adele Shepard Collection of Edwards and Dwight Family Papers
Adele Shepard Collection of Edwards and Dwight Family Papers
titlestmt.author: Martha Lund Smalley

controlnote.p: This collection contains 1 published digital object. And that object are comprised of 31 digital files.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 8, 2025 at 18:15
Finding aid revised following receipt of approximately 150 additional items from Adele Shepard.

did.unittitle: Adele Shepard Collection of Edwards and Dwight Family Papers
did.abstract: This collection of Edwards and Dwight family papers was inherited and compiled by Adele Shepard, a descendant of the families. The papers focus primarily on the individuals in Shepard's line of descent, including Jonathan Edwards, his grandson Timothy Dwight (1752-1817), great-grandson William T. Dwight, great-great-grandson Henry E. Dwight and their spouses and children. The collection also includes other documents and photographs of historical interest with direct or tangential relation to the Edwards and Dwight families.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Numerous members of the Edwards and Dwight families are represented in this collection. The primary focus of the records is on the direct line of descent from Jonathan Edwards (1703-1758) to the donor of the collection, Adele Shepard:
Jonathan Edwards, the son of Timothy Edwards (1669-1758), married Sarah Pierpont (1710-1758).
Jonathan and Sarah Pierpont Edwards' daughter Mary Edwards (1734-1807) married Timothy Dwight (1726-1777), who was the son of Timothy Dwight (1694-1771).

The first son of Timothy and Mary Edwards Dwight was Timothy Dwight (1752-1817), who was President of Yale College from 1795 to 1817. Timothy Dwight (1752-1817) married Mary Woolsey (1754-1845) and among their six sons was William Theodore Dwight (1795-1865) who served as minister at the Third Congregational Church of Portland, Maine from 1832 to 1864. Another son was Sereno Edwards Dwight (1786-1850) who served as minister at the Park Street Church in Boston and as President of Hamilton College.
William T. Dwight married Elizabeth Loockerman Bradford (1810-1863), daughter of Elizabeth and Thomas Bradford.
William and Elizabeth Dwight's son Henry Edwin Dwight, M.D. (1832-1908) married Laure (Laura) Emma Lehmann (Leman) (1836-1928), who was originally from Neuchatel, Switzerland.
The second daughter of Henry E. and Laura Lehmann Dwight was Adele Mary Dwight (1869-1923), who married F. Lynwood Garrison (1862-1951).
F. Lynwood and Adele Dwight Garrison's daughter Elizabeth Dwight Garrison (1906-1996) married Charles Shepard (1900-1989).
Charles and Elizabeth Garrison Shepard's daughter Adele Elizabeth Shepard inherited the papers in the collection and presented them to the Yale Divinity School Library in 2018.

Because the materials in this collection were largely gathered and preserved by the women who married into the Dwight family, there is significant documentation of the Bradford and Lehmann families. Thomas Bradford, father of Elizabeth Bradford Dwight (1810-1863) and a descendant of Philadelphia printer William Bradford (1663-1752), was born in 1781, and attended the University of Pennsylvania. He subsequently became a prestigious lawyer in the Philadelphia area and married Elizabeth Loockerman, daughter of wealthy landowner Vincent Loockerman of Delaware. Laure (Laura) Emma Lehmann, wife of Henry E. Dwight, M.D. (1832-1908), was born in Switzerland. A genealogical chart for the Lehmann family is available in Series IV.

For additional biographical information, see Series IV, Biographical Documentation. Some biographical information is also provided throughout the finding aid in order to define the individuals mentioned.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The material is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: III. Financial, Legal, and Church Records, 1685-1867.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of correspondence, writings, sermons, financial and legal documents, photographs, and other memorabilia of Jonathan Edwards and Timothy Dwight and their descendants. This collection of Edwards and Dwight family papers was inherited and compiled by Adele Shepard, a descendant of the families. The papers focus primarily on the individuals in Shepard's line of descent, including Jonathan Edwards (1703-1758), Edwards' grandson Timothy Dwight (1752-1817) and his wife Mary Woolsey Dwight, great-grandson William T. Dwight and his wife Elizabeth Bradford Dwight, great-great-grandson Henry E. Dwight and his wife Laura Lehmann Dwight, and other descendants. The collection also includes other materials of historical interest with direct or tangential relation to the Edwards and Dwight families. The Shepard Collection complements other papers held at Yale, including the Dwight Family Papers (MS 187), Jonathan Edwards Collection (GEN MSS 151), and Andover Newton Theological Seminary Collection of Jonathan Edwards (GEN MSS 1542).

The collection includes original documents by Jonathan Edwards, including a manuscript notebook containing descriptions of the ecstatic experiences of his wife Sarah Pierpont Edwards (1710-1758). Versions of this narrative were published by Jonathan Edwards in Some Thoughts Concerning the Revivals (1743) and by Sereno Edwards Dwight in The Works of President Edwards (1829) but these were altered so as to conceal Sarah's identity or the extent and intensity of her experiences.

Many of the most interesting documents in this collection are by or about women, including Jonathan Edwards sister Mary Edwards' memorial of their sister Jerusha and extensive correspondence of Elizabeth Bradford Dwight, wife of William T. Dwight. Of particular note is a letter written from Montreal by Ester (Esther/Hester) Ingersoll Gurley Jones (1665-1705) after she had been taken captive by the French and Indians from a village near Northhampton, MA during Queen Anne's War.

The collection includes letters from a number of notable correspondents, including John Adams (1735-1826), Lyman Beecher (1775-1863), John Trumbull (1756-1843), and George Washington. The active intellectual exchange of Timothy Dwight (1752-1817) with other figures in the U.S. and Europe is reflected in the correspondence. Letters from Dwight to his wife Mary Woolsey Dwight during the Revolutionary War document his work as an army chaplain while letters to and from his sons, particularly William T. Dwight (1795-1865) and Sereno Edwards Dwight (1786-1850), reflect the close ties of the family.

In Series I, the correspondence of Edwards and Dwight family members is arranged according to generations, with a focus on the particular branches of the families most thoroughly documented in this collection. This section is followed by correspondence of other individuals, not to or from Edwards or Dwight family members, which was collected by the families.

Series II, Writings and Notes, is also divided into two sections: writings and notes of Edwards and Dwight family members, and items authored by non-family members. Sermon notes of Jonathan Edwards' father Timothy Edwards (1669-1758) are included as well as unpublished writings of Timothy Dwight (1752-1817) and Sereno Edwards Dwight (1786-1850), who was pastor of the Park Street Church, Boston from 1817 to 1826 and president of Hamilton College from 1833 to 1835 before resigning because of ill health due to accidental mercury poisoning. A number of the published sermons by others in this Series relate to the issue of whether a man can marry his deceased wife's sister or his brother's widow, issues that had been addressed by Sereno Edwards Dwight when he was practicing law, prior to becoming a minister.

Series III, Financial, Legal, and Church Records, contains an eclectic group of documents dating from 1685 to 1867, including materials related to the work of Timothy Dwight (1694-1771) as a Justice of the Peace in Massachusetts. Also included is a receipt for the sale of an enslaved boy named Titus to Jonathan Edwards by Hezekiah Griswold.

Series IV, Biographical Documentation, includes extensive genealogical information regarding the Edwards and Dwight families, compiled by Adele Shepard and others, as well as collected documentation arranged by individual.

The Photographs and Artwork of Series V include a very interesting collection of family portraits in evolving photographic formats, including daguerreotypes, ambrotypes, tintypes, and cartes-de-visite as well as photographic prints.

Series VI, Collected Materials and Artifacts, contains an eclectic mix of documents and items collected by the Dwight family, including a 1780 printed broadside in which New Haven native and former Continental Army leader Benedict Arnold explains why he has decided "to join the King's arms," regarding the revolutionary leaders as "Usurpers" and "criminals" who are serving their own interests.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Adele Shepard Collection of Edwards and Dwight Family Papers (RG 329), Special Collections, Yale Divinity Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Timothy Edwards (1669-1758) was the father of twelve children, including Jonathan Edwards (1703-1758)

did.unittitle: To his mother Esther Stoddard Edwards (1672-1771)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Addressed from Yale College when Edwards was working on his master's degree.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This letter marks the initiation of Edwards' correspondence with John Erskine, minister and revivalist of Kirkintilloch, which would continue to the end of Edwards' life and was later maintained by Edwards' son Jonathan Edwards, Jr.

did.unittitle: To Joseph Dwight (drafts of four letters)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bridgadier Joseph Dwight was a a Trustee of "the Indian Schools," a position he held when Jonathan Edwards was also at work there as a missionary (1751-1758).

did.unittitle: To son Timothy Edwards (1738-1813) - handwritten copies of two letters
To her sister Mary Edwards (4 letters)
To her sister Mary Edwards (4 letters)
To her sister Jerusha Edwards (2 letters)
To her parents Timothy and Esther Stoddard Edwards
To his grandmother Esther Stoddard Edwards
To his sister Mary Edwards Dwight
To John Erskine and unidentified fragment (possibly to Erskine)
See "Correspondence of Dwight-Edwards family members" subseries

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mother of Timothy Dwight (1694-1771); also known as Mehetable

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1741 letter relates to Timothy Dwight's son's program at Yale College and has notes by Dwight on the verso.

did.unittitle: From [Thomas] Pownall, Governor of the the Province of Massachusetts Bay

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed letter ordering enforcement of recruitment act.

did.unittitle: Letter fragment, signed by Timothy Dwight
Letter from Daniel Dwight (?) to unidentified correspondent

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written from "Boston Goal"

did.unittitle: See "Correspondence of Dwight-Edwards family members" subseries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes correspondence of Timothy Dwight (1726-1777) and his wife Mary Edwards Dwight and their descendants, including their son Timothy Dwight (1752-1817), his wife Mary Woolsey Dwight, his siblings, and his children.

did.unittitle: To his son Timothy Dwight (1752-1817), tutor at Yale College (two letters)
From son T. Dwight (Theodore or Timothy)
To son Timothy Dwight and his wife Mary Woolsey Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Provides information about her grandfather Timothy Edwards.

did.unittitle: To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight (1734-1807) - fragment
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight (3 letters)
To his wife Mary Woolsey Dwight (1754-1845)
To his wife Mary Woolsey Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 page letter (6 leaves) - Dwight is with the Continental Army in the New York Highlands

did.unittitle: To his wife Mary Woolsey Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 page letter (6 leaves) - Dwight describes hike up Sugarloaf Mountain in New York.

did.unittitle: To his wife Mary Woolsey Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 page letter lacks conclusion - Dwight describes matters relating to the Revolutionary War.

did.unittitle: To his wife Mary Woolsey Dwight
To his wife Mary Woolsey Dwight (three letters, one fragmentary)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These letters were written during the Revolutionary War. In the letter dated October 13 Dwight reports that George Washington attended his service and dined with him. The letter of February 28 was written from the camp of the Continental Army. Dwight recounts the army's latest movements and actions and describes the Hudson River Valley.

did.unittitle: To his son William T. Dwight
To his son William T. Dwight, with enclosed undated note to Timothy Dwight from Erasmus Darwin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letter to William T. Dwight lacks its concluding page(s).

did.unittitle: To his son William T. Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vice President of the United States at the time, John Adams (1735-1826) writes expressing his appreciation for receiving a copy of Dwight's Greenfield Hill (1794), an idyll describing the towns and villages of New England.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from E. Darwin, physician at Derby, England, regarding Dwight's work "The Conquest of Canaan."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on back: "Letter to Doctor Darwin - Imperfect Draught" (7 pages)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Verso contains sermon notes by Dwight on Proverbs 28.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of letter by Josiah Pratt, Society for Missions to Africa and the East, London.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding placement of Williams College. (10 pages)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written from New Haven.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written from Miami Rapids, Headquarters of the Northwestern Army, during War of 1812.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Robert Stuart was pastor of the Walnut Hill church near Lexington, Kentucky

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writing from his home in Mt. Vernon, Washington thanks Timothy Dwight, whom he would have known as a chaplain in the Continental Army during the Revolutionary War, for sending a copy of Dwight's newly published epic poem "The Conquest of Canaan" (1785), which portrayed the taking of Connecticut from the British as a biblical allegory.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1799 letter relates to Joel Barlow.

did.unittitle: Two unidentified fragments from Timothy Dwight
Two letter wrappers addressed to Timothy Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One wrapper has a wax seal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Copy of letter by Dr. Dwight regarding student (Mr. Nicoll) sent down from Yale College, by Edward Huntington (?)
From William Ellsworth of behalf of Yale Class of 1810 - tribute to President Timothy Dwight
To son William T. Dwight (3 letters)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter is signed M. W. Dwight and speaks of Lizzie, Egbert, and Henry; identity of author unclear.

did.unittitle: To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To her mother Mary Edwards Dwight (?), from "S.D."
To her mother Mary Woolsey Dwight
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To his sister Sally Dwight (2 letters)
To his brother Timothy Dwight (1752-1817) - fragment
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight
To her mother Mary Edwards Dwight (3 letters)
To his mother Mary Edwards Dwight (4 letters)
Children of Timothy Dwight (1752-1817) and Mary Woolsey Dwight
Timothy Dwight (1778-1844) and his wife Clarissa
Clarissa Dwight to her brother-in-law William T. Dwight
Timothy Dwight (1778-1844) to his brother William T. Dwight (two letters)
To his mother Mary Woolsey Dwight
To his brother William T. Dwight
To his brother William T. Dwight
From his step-grandmother Anne Muirson Woolsey
From Edward Everett - letter cover only

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter relates to Sereno Edwards Dwight's dispute with Leonard Woods and includes a note from Gerrit Smith.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter relates to Sereno Edwards Dwight's dispute with Leonard Woods

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters exchanged between Dwight and Woods during 1827-1829 relate to a dispute they were having. Allegations of falsehood and slander on various matters were exchanged. Woods wished to convene a tribunal of referees to adjudicate the issues. Letters from Dwight to Woods in this sequence are in some cases drafts, with phrases crossed out and corrected, and in other cases are copies made by Henry E. Dwight.

did.unittitle: Printed invitation to Yale University Junior Exhibition
See "Correspondence of Dwight-Bradford family members" subseries,
To his brother William T Dwight (seven letters)
To his brother William T. Dwight and sister-in-law Elizabeth Bradford Dwight (nine letters)
To his mother Mary Woolsey Dwight
To his brother Benjamin Woolsey Dwight (6 letters)
To his brother Benjamin Woolsey Dwight (6 letters)
To his brother Benjamin Woolsey Dwight (6 letters)
To his brother Benjamin Woolsey Dwight (6 letters)
To his brother Benjamin Woolsey Dwight (6 letters)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1842 letter includes note to daughter Mary from Elizabeth Bradford Dwight as well as William T. Dwight's letter to his brother.

did.unittitle: To his sister-in-law Clarissa Dwight (5 letters)
To his sister-in-law Clarissa Dwight (6 letters)

To his son Henry E. Dwight (6 letters)
To his daughter Elizabeth Bradford Dwight (Lizzy)
From nephews Edward Strong Dwight and James Dwight (3 letters)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter relates to memoir of Wm. T. Dwight's father Timothy Dwight

did.unittitle: From S.B. Chittenden, Treasurer of Central Committee for Congregational Yearbook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Silliman regarding the formation of the Yale Alumni Society with enclosure of typed transcript of original and a letter from Effingham B. Morris, Jr to F. Lynwood Garrison returning the Silliman letter.

From her grandmother Elizabeth Nixon and her grandfather (3 letters)
To her parents Thomas and Elizabeth Bradford (3 letters)
To her husband William T. Dwight (2 letters)
To her son Henry Edwin Dwight (11 letters)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters contain notes from Henry's brother Thomas, sister Mary, and grandfather Thomas Bradford

did.unittitle: To her son Thomas Bradford Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters to uncle Nicholas Loockerman, from aunt Mary Goldsborough, from cousin Louisa Goldsborough, to/from aunt Mary Bayley, from brothers Vincent and Thomas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Susan Dwight, Jacob and Mary Ritter, Clarissa Dwight, cousin T. D. Porter

did.unittitle: From cousin Eliza B. Wallace (11 letters)
To/from various friends and acquaintances (8 letters)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Amanda Donaldson, Mrs. H. Grubb, Charlotte Hannum, Mary Lippincott (friendship token of painted flowers), Mrs. Payson, Sarah Rogers, Sarah Stille

did.unittitle: Parents and siblings of Elizabeth Bradford Dwight
Thomas Bradford to his daughter Elizabeth Bradford Dwight (11 letters)

Elizabeth Loockerman Bradford and Thomas Bradford to/from family members (6 letters)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including to/from Elizabeth's mother Elizabeth Nixon, sister M. Nixon, brother Nicholas Loockerman. Letter of 1819 includes a letter from Elizabeth Bradford (Dwight) to her grandmother Elizabeth Nixon.

scopecontent.p: The 1844 letter contains a note from Dwight's grandfather, Thomas Bradford, to Dwight's mother.

To his sister Elizabeth Bradford Dwight (Smyth)

To his wife Laura Emma Lehman (Leman) Dwight (3 letters)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from his cousins Sophia, A. D. Hooker, and Charles Townsend, uncle Benjamin Rush Bradford, James Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including from William Carruthers, from Frederick Oxnard, from Mary Burchard (letter in German), call to pastorate First Church, Randolph MA,

did.unittitle: Laura Emma Lehman (Leman) Dwight (wife of Henry E. Dwight)
Letters in French from mother and other relatives and friends (16 letters)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letters are written primarily from Neuchatel, Switzerland and addressed to "Emma." A few letters are addressed to "Mes cheres soeurs" or "Adele."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letters are written primarily from Neuchatel, Switzerland and addressed to "Emma." A few letters are addressed to "Laure."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letters are written primarily from Neuchatel, Switzerland and addressed to "Emma." A few letters are addressed to "Laure" or "Charlie."

scopecontent.p: The letters are written primarily from Neuchatel, Switzerland and addressed to "Emma."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letters are written primarily from Neuchatel, Switzerland and addressed to "Emma." A few letters are addressed to "Laure" or "Adele."

Letters in English from Sophie Patten, Mary Portman, Lissie Smyth, George Smyth (5 letters)
Enclosures with letters to Laura Emma Dwight - poems, drawings, recipe, photos
Enclosures with letters to Laura Emma Dwight - accounts, visiting cards, invitations, envelopes

scopecontent.p: One letter contains note from her sister Mary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This may be from Timothy Dwight 18828-1916, son of Elizabeth Bradford Dwight Smyth's uncle James Dwight.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letter relates to the deciphering of Jonathan Edwards' shorthand.

did.unittitle: To his mother, to "My dear sister," to sister Mary, to sister Elizabeth and her husband Egbert, and to "Lissie" (6 letters)
To her brother Henry E. Dwight
Children of Henry E. and Laura E. Dwight
From cousin William Trautwine; from Edward Partridge (2 letters)
Letters in French, addressed from Neuchatel, Switzerland (3 letters)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From "Tante Laure" and Therese Sandoz

did.unittitle: From John Chester Lyman, re. death of "Cousin Lizzie" (Elizabeth Bradford Dwight Smyth)
From cousin Timothy Dwight (1828-1916) re. the death of his daughter Helen
From G. E. Thompson, Yale Alumni University Fund Association
Printed invitations and calling card with note

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1903 invitation, addressed to Adele and her husband F. Lynwood Garrison, is for Bicentenary of the Birth of Jonathan Edward, ceremony to be held at Andover Theological Seminary.

did.unittitle: Children of Adele Dwight Garrison and F. Lynwood Garrison
From Dwight Garrison (1896-) to his sister Laura (1904-)
Ester [Esther / Hester] Jones to her son Samuel Gurley, to be conveyed by Pelatiah Jones

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Esther [or Hester] Ingersoll Gurley Jones (1665-1705), born in Northampton, MA, first married William Gurley (1665-1687) in 1684 and then Benoni Jones (1665-1704) in 1689. She was the mother of Samuel Gurley (1687-1760) of Coventry, Conn. Pelatiah Jones (1664-1739), of Springfield, was her brother-in-law. On May 13, 1704, during Queen Anne's War, the French and Indians made a raid upon Pascommuck (a remote hamlet of Northampton). Of the 33 inhabitants of the village, 19 were killed and 6 taken captive (3 of whom escaped). Benoni Jones and the two youngest children of the family were among those killed. Esther was one of those taken captive and marched on foot to Canada, where, reportedly, she was cruelly treated and the French tried to force her convert to Catholicism. She died in Montreal, and is buried in the graveyard of the Hotel de Dieu. This letter was written from Canada shortly before her death on November 27, 1705.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sally Prince was a friend of Esther Edwards Burr

did.unittitle: John Brainerd to Eleazar Wheelock (7 letters, one fragment); fragment of letter from Brainerd to unidentified correspondent

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Brainerd, younger brother of David Brainerd, was a missionary to Native Americans in New Jersey from 1747 to 1781. At the time of this correspondence Eleazar Wheelock had founded a school for Native Americans in Lebanon, Connecticut.

did.unittitle: Aaron Burr (1756-1836) to unidentified correspondents (2 fragments)
Lyman Beecher (1775-1863) to his mother Roxanna Foote

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letter is an introduction for Sereno Edwards Dwight upon his visit to Europe for his health.

did.unittitle: Gilbert du Motier, Marquis de Lafayette, to "Gentlemen of the Committee of Arrangement"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letter relates to arrangements for an Independence Day dinner.

did.unittitle: Andrew Jackson (1767-1845) to General George Gibson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This note may be from Catharine Maria Sedgwick (1789-1867), an American novelist who was instrumental in the development of domestic fiction. She was a daughter of General Joseph Dwight and was born and buried in Stockbridge, MA.

did.unittitle: "To my dear sir" written from New Haven - letter draft (?)
Jonathan (? Savory) to Rev. Mr. Dwight
Letter cover "To His Excellency Conduriote President of the Government of Greece Napoli di Romania"
Sermon notes on Psalm 15:4 (1 leaf - fragment)
Notebook: notes on theological subjects and quotations (paginated 3-136)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: When Jonathan Edwards' sister Jerusha Edwards died in 1729 of diptheria at age nineteen, father Timothy Edwards asked several of his daughters to write their reminiscences of her character and piety, probably with the intention fo publishing a eulogistic pamphlet. In the end only an obituary was published in a newspaper, but several of these sisterly memorials have survived, including this one by Mary Edwards. See also Yale's Beinecke Library GEN MSS 151 and 1542.

did.unittitle: Bound manuscript sermon on Canticles 1:12 (16 pp.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sermon, dating from the height of the Connecticut River Valley Awakening, was preached on a day when the sacrament of the Lord's Supper was observed. Based on one of Edwards' favorite books of the Bible (Cantitles, also known as Song of Solomon) dwells on the communion that believers have with Christ. Possibly his daughter Mary Edwards Dwight chose this piece, dating from the year in which she was born, out of her father's manuscripts when he died, as a remembrance of him.

did.unittitle: Sermon notes by Edwards written on verso of a banns of marriage for a Mohican couple in the town of Stockbridge signed by Timothy Woodbridge
Manuscript notebook: "Mrs. Edwards' Experiences in Jan. 19 &c 1742"

descriptivenote.p: Manuscript notebook: "Mrs. Edwards' Experiences in Jan. 19 &c 1742"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This manuscript contains descriptions of the ecstatic experiences of Sarah Pierpont Edwards (1710-1758) as related to and recorded by her husband Jonathan Edwards. Versions of this narrative were published by Jonathan Edwards in Some Thoughts Concerning the Revivals (1743) and by Sereno Edwards Dwight in The Works of President Edwards (1829) but these were altered so as to conceal Sarah's identity or the extent and intensity of her experiences.

did.unittitle: "President Edwards' Resolutions and Diary with Mrs. Edwards Diary" (manuscript copy - incomplete)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This copy includes "Resolutions" 1-70 and diary entries for 1722 December 18 to 1723 January 1.

did.unittitle: Manuscript copies of prayers and "Confession of Sin" (6 leaves)
Reviews and clippings re. Observations concerning the Scripture oeconomy of the Trinity and covenant of redemption - Edwards manuscript edited and published by Egbert C. Smyth
Printed sermon: A Sermon delivered November 5, 1783 at the Ordination of the Reverend Mr. Timothy Dwight
Printed sermon: The Marriage of a Wife's Sister Considered in a Sermon delivered in the Chapel of Yale College (2 copies)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Some writings in this section have been attributed to Timothy Dwight but authorship is unclear.

did.unittitle: "Memoir of the life of President Dwight" (pp. 1-60 + 9 leaves)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten document with pages numbered 1-60 and additional unnumbered pages, some fragmentary

did.unittitle: "Theology: Explained and Defended, in a Series of Sermons; by Timothy Dwight S.T.D., L.L.D, Late President of Yale College with a Memoir of the Life of the Author in Five Volumes - Vol. 1" - fragmentary draft

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This fragmentary draft contains pages 62-114 and has many cross-outs. The title page of the manuscript says "Middletown, Printed by Clark and Lyman for Timothy Dwight, New Haven, 1818."

did.unittitle: Pages from memoir of Timothy Dwight with cover letter from T. Dwight, New York
"A Sketch of the Life & Character of President Dwight" (30 pp)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This handwritten document is a draft with many cross-outs. There are 7 numbered leaves and additional pages interspersed.

did.unittitle: Bound booklet: manuscript sermon on Eccles. 9:12
did.unitdate: Undated, reference on cover to 1812 and 1815

did.unittitle: Bound booklet: manuscript sermon on Matthew 13:52
Bound booklet: "A Discourse , preached on the Annual Thanksgiving"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The sermon is on Psalm 13:6. Notation on cover: "Copied TD Jr"

did.unittitle: Sermon notes (?) - on verso of hand drawn map "Plot of Lyman Swamp"
Sheet with various manuscript poems, including "The Adieu" and "Kate of Aberdeen"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 'Kate of Aberdeen' was written by John Cunningham (1729-1773), a Dublin playwright, poet and actor. "The Adieu" is by Lord Byron.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Authorship unclear

did.unittitle: Bound manuscript hymnbook with musical notation (pp. 13-69); one loose leaf of musical notation titled "Wordsworth L.U."

title.part: Sermon, preached at Northampton, on the twenty-eighth of November, 1781 : occasioned by the capture of the British army, under the command of Earl Cornwallis

did.unittitle: The Folly, Guilt, and Mischiefs of Duelling : a sermon, preached in the College Chapel at New Haven, on the Sabbath preceding the annual commencement, September, 1804 (2 copies)

title.part: Sermon Preached at the Opening of the Theological Institution in Andover, and at the ordination of Rev. Eliphalet Pearson, LL.D
The Charitable Blessed : a sermon, preached in the First Church in New-Haven
A Statistical Account of the City of New-Haven

did.unittitle: Bound notebook of Yale College compositions
Appendices for "Life of President Edwards" including "The Mind"
Manuscript fragment - description of last days of an eminent saint (pp. 13-20)
Compilation of manuscript poems in classical mode; Manuscript notes (2 leaves); Essays: "A question of casuistry", "Response to a reviewer"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Authorship unclear

did.unittitle: Compilation of sermons and other writings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Authorship unclear

did.unittitle: Cover sheet only for "Minutes of the occurrences of my Father's last illness..."
Report of committee of Third Congregational Church in Portland, Maine re. Sabbath violations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Authorship unclear

did.unittitle: Notes on the history of Greenfield Hill

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Authorship unclear

did.unittitle: Printed address: Address delivered at the Forty-fifth Anniversary of the American Tract Society
Manuscript essay: "The History of the Bible"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adele Lehmann was the sister of Laura Emma Lehmann Dwight and sister-in-law of Henry E. Dwight, M.D.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily in French.

did.unittitle: "Receipt" for the "Phanaticall Diers" cordial by Richard Daniels

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Doodles by Timothy Edwards on verso

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes booklet of sermons addressed to Rev. James Pierpont Pastor of the Church of Christ at New Haven, booklet containing two sermons on Deuteronomy 32:29, and a sermon outline (1 leaf)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including biography of Habijah Weld, fragment of address related to Harvard College, two essays in German titled "Ausgabe..."

did.unittitle: Notebook and note fragment by unidentified authors

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The notebook may relate to a trip to Italy; the note may relate to Yale, mentions Professor Fitch.

did.unittitle: Biographical sketch of Timothy Dwight (1752-1817) and notes on his life by unidentified authors
Notes by unidentified authors: On pronunciation in English, Glory of God, Trinity
Jonathan Bartlett: Remarks on the Question, Is it lawful to marry the sister of a deceased wife? ...
Lyman Beecher: The Bible a Code of Laws : a sermon, delivered in Park Street Church, Boston

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sermon delivered at the ordination of Sereno Edwards Dwight.

did.unittitle: Benjamin Colman: The Faithful Ministers of Christ Mindful of their own Death: A sermon preached at the lecture in Boston; upon the death of the learned and venerable Solomon Stoddard late Pastor of the Church of Christ in Northampton
Daniel Dana: A Sermon on the Atonement : preached at the annual convention of the Congregational and Presbyterian ministers of the state of New Hampshire

James Dana: An Examination of the late Reverend President Edward's 'Enquiry on freedom of will : more especially the foundation principle of his book, with the tendency and consequences of the reasoning therein contained ...

Edward Dorr: A Discourse, occasioned by the much lamented death, of the Honorable Daniel Edwards, Esq; of Hartford : a member of His Majesty's Council, for the colony of Connecticut; and one of the assistant judges of the Honorable, the Superior Court, for said colony
Nathanael Emmons: A Discourse, delivered at the installation of the Rev. Thomas Williams, at Foxborough

Roswell Judson: Two epistles of Free Stricture, on the American dictionary of Mr. Webster, on the Hebrew grammar and Hebrew chrestomathy of Mr. Stuart, and on the manual Hebrew lexicon of Mr. Gibbs: : addressed to Rev. Eleazar T. Fitch, D.D. divinity professor in Yale College
William Miltimore: Sermon preached March 18, 1812, at the installation of the Rev. Elijah Kellogg : to the pastoral care of the Chapel Congregational Church and Society in Portland
Nehemiah Prudden: To Marry a Wife's Sister: not inconsistent with the divine law. To which is added, some remarks on Dr. Trumbull's late appeal to the public
Egbert C. Smyth: Sketch of Rev. Seth Sweetser
Benjamin Trumbull: An Appeal to the Public, relative to the unlawfulness of marrying a wife's sister. [Part I-II]

Benjamin Trumbull: An Appeal to the Public, especially to the learned, with respect to the unlawfulness of divorces, in all cases, excepting those of incontinency: The substance of the argument was pleaded before the Consociation of the County of New-Haven, December 9th, 1785

Benjamin Trumbull: What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder: an appeal to the public with respect to the unlawfulness of divorces .. pleaded before the Consociation of the county of New-Haven, December 9th, 1785 / by Benjamin Trumbull ; to which are added extracts from Dr. Dwight's sermon on divorce.
Stephen West: The Duty and Obligation of Christians to Marry only in the Lord, illustrated and urged
Stephen West: A Vindication of the Principles and Conduct of the Church in Stockbridge, in excluding from their communion, one of their members, for marrying a person immoral and profane
Anonymous: A Treatise on the Unlawfulness of Marrying a Brother's Wife: together with remarks on the marriage of a wife's sister
Anonymous: A Consideration of the Right of Marrying the Sister of a Deceased Wife, or the wife of a Deceased Brother : also, of the propriety of making the practice a matter of Christian discipline

title.part: Fornication Binds the Criminal Parties to Marry: the decision of the Congregational Church in Rupert, Vt., relative to a case of discipline

did.unittitle: Copy of ruling by General Court for Elections held at Boston, MA regarding setting boundaries of Northampton, MA
Agreement for Nathaniel Dwight and Samuel Partridge to pay Simeon Stoddard a sum of money
Testimony of Nathaniel Dwight to ecclesiastical council regarding Thomas Shelding
Accounting of debts due to the estate of Nathaniel Dwight and Inventory of the estate
Ruling of Judge Samuel Partridge settling land on Timothy Dwight
Codicil to will of Nathaniel Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy made by Timothy Dwight (1694-1771)? Nathaniel Dwight died in 1711.

did.unittitle: Receipt for payment to Timothy Dwight from his mother Mehitable Dwight, administrator of estate of Nathaniel Dwight
Deed of land from Nathaniel Phelps to Timothy Dwight
Account of funds due to soldiers recruited by Timothy Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: During Father Rale's War (1722-1725), one of a series of conflicts between the English and the Abenaki Timothy Dwight (1694-1771) recruited and commanded a contingent of soldiers, fo rwhom he kep a record of the funds due them for their services. Written over these financial records are notes on sermons, beginning with one by Solomon Stoddard, minister of Northampton, MA, in 1726

did.unittitle: Receipt for payment by Asaph Leavitt to Samuel Dwight (1696-1763)
Acquittance of Nathaniel Dwight from Timothy Dwight
Deed for sale of land by Caleb Bull to Jonathan Dwight
Arrest warrant for Joseph Stebbins signed by John Stoddard, with settlement by Timothy Dwight, Justice of the Peace
Testimony vs. Rev. Benjamin Doolittle and copy of Rev. Doolittle's first answer (regarding Northfield, MA controversy)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the early 1740s, some of the members of the Northfield, Mass., church complained about their minister, Benjamin Doolittle, who opposed the revivals then occurring. Other papers relating to this controversy are in the other collections of Edwards Papers at Yale's Beinecke Library (GEN MSS 151 and 1542).

did.unittitle: Court decisions of Timothy Dwight (1694-1771), Justice of the Peace

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Judgment vs. Thankfull Frost" and regarding Daniel Woods.

did.unittitle: Testimonies of John Moulton and Samuel Moulton of Brimfield regarding libel charges
Timothy Dwight (1694-1771) petition to Governor Francis Barnard regarding position as Justice of the Peace
Copy of Hampshire Association of Ministers decision regarding Oliver Warner

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Oliver Warner was requesting restoration of membership in the Northampton Church. A note in Jonathan Edwards' handwriting on the verso reads "Association advice concerning Oliver Warner."

did.unittitle: Notification of meeting called by the General Court of the Province of the Massachusetts Bay of proprietors of land in Stockbridge

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed by Timothy Dwight.

did.unittitle: Timothy Dwight's protest of decision of council of churches to dismiss Jonathan Edwards from his pastorate in Northampton
Court decision records of Timothy Dwight, including order to arrest Daniel Sitton for debts
Receipt for sale of enslaved boy named Titus to Jonathan Edwards by Hezekiah Griswold
Bill from "The Contractors Victualing His Majesty's Forces in North America" to Timothy Dwight (1726-1777)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Witness signatures include Mary Dwight and Timothy Dwight (1726-1777)

did.unittitle: Receipt for boarding fees of Sereno Edwards Dwight 1772-1774
Military service certification for Thomas Wallis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This manuscript note, signed by local commanders, certifies the service of Thomas Wallis, a Connecticut Minuteman, in the conflicts around Boston in the spring of 1775.

did.unittitle: Certification of membership in the Church of Christ at Yale for Timothy Dwight, signed by Samuel Wales
Document conveying Power of Attorney for Timothy Dwight to Henry Porter
Deposition of Timothy Dwight regarding debt owed by Moses Grave to his father Timothy Dwight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes note from T. Dwight to Sally Dwight dated 1793 November 17.

did.unittitle: Certification by John Rodgers regarding General Charles Pinckney's "belief in Revelation"
Manuscript copy of Act to Incorporate the American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions, with cover letter addressed from Charlestown
Report of Ecclesiastical Council convened at Littleton, MA to consider case of Mrs. Lucy Dix
London publisher's proposal to publish sermons of Dr. Dwight
Bound ledger of William T. Dwight containing record of household expenses and brief entries regarding weather and activities
Monetary statement related to lawsuit against D. Appleton and Co. publishers
Documents related to estate of Timothy Dwight (1752-1817) and his wife
Will of Elizabeth Bradford Dwight; Inventory of estate of William Bradford, and other legal records and notes
Document conveying Power of Attorney for Egbert C. and Elizabeth B. D. Smyth to Thomas Bradford Dwight
Notes regarding Edwards and Dwight family genealogies
Narrative and notes related to Edwards family genealogy; cover letter by Tryon Edwards to unidentified correspondent

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: It is not certain that the cover letter refers to the genealogical document.

did.unittitle: Bound notebook containing genealogical information about Dwight family, copied by Henry E. Dwight
Notes regarding Dwight family genealogy by Sereno Edwards Dwight
Letter from James Dwight regarding Dwight family in South Carolina
"Memoranda of Sereno concerning the Isaacs family"
Newspaper clipping, booklet and sketch related to Dwight family coat of arms
The History of the Descendants of John Dwight of Dedham, Mass. by Benjamin W. Dwight (2 volumes)
Genealogical chart of Lehmann family of Neuchatel, compiled by Charles Shepard
Jonathan Edwards issue of The Christian World - The Congregationalist and newspaper clippings related to Edwards' bicentennial; two photos of Edwards' shorthand
"The Edwards Stock" - article in The Nation by William H. Powers; clipping of address by Dr. E.A. Park mentioning Edwards
Boston Gazette , no. 1586 - reporting result of Council dismissing Jonathan Edwards and Protest of the minority
Minute of parish meeting in Charlestown Church calling Timothy Dwight to become pastor
Call of Timothy Dwight to be pastor of Dutch Reformed Church of Albany, NY
Printed broadside "Hymns to be sung on the evening of February 5, 1817 occasioned by the death of the Rev. Timothy Dwight"
"When Timothy Dwight Came to Yale" - article in The Congregationalist and Christian World
Handwritten note indicating that she received a pension from the government as a Revolutionary widow
Papers related to his dismissal as pastor of Park Street Church, Boston
Clipping related to Yale College Crew team of 1854; clippings related to Dwight's death and funeral
Program from reunion of Yale Class of 1852; clipping related to Dwight's funeral
Certificate of membership in Maine Missionary Society; clipping related to presentation of portrait of her father to Congregational library ; cliippings related to her death
Program from presentation of his portrait at Andover Theological Seminary; article in Boston Evening Transcript following his death
Issue of The Legal Intelligencer , published in Philadelphia - containing tributes to Thomas Bradford Dwight
Application for membership in The Pennsylvania Society of the Colonial Dames of America

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Son of Timothy Dwight (1828-1916)

did.unittitle: Announcement of his marriage to Mary Ketcham Talmage
Obituary of Benjamin R. Bradford; notice regarding marriage of Joan Bradford
Family history materials collected by Adele Shepard
Contents of notebook compiled by Adele Shepard, including genealogical notes, photographs
Contents of notebook compiled by Adele Shepard, including genealogical notes, Internet printouts
Contents of notebook labeled "Sharon K.'s family"
Family history material compiled by Adele Shepard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including descendants of Henry E. and Laura Dwight.

scopecontent.p: 5 copies of headshot portrait, 2 copies of standing portrait, 1 copy of alternate standing portrait

scopecontent.p: Wife of William T. Dwight.

scopecontent.p: Six cards with headshots at varying ages (duplicates of two); three cards with standing pose (duplicate of 1)

did.unittitle: Two larger size prints on card stock; one poor quality negative

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Tintype - Elizabeth "Lily" as small child in standing pose
Tintypes - 2 poses; taken by famed American photographer Frederick Gutekunst, Philadelpha

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One photograph is black and white and the other is framed and colored.

did.unittitle: Cartes de visite (6; 2 duplicates; 2 with sister Adele)
Larger format photographs on card stock (6; portraits at various ages)
Larger format photographs on card stock (9; 3 poses) - in costume as "Romanian bride"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Second daughter of Henry E. and Laura Dwight.

did.unittitle: Tintypes - 2 poses; taken by famed American photographer Frederick Gutekunst, Philadelphia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The folder includes a note explaining how these photos of "Innocence or Little May Blossom" became well known. Three of the photos are black and white and the third is colored.

did.unittitle: Cartes de visite (8 cards, one print; 4 poses, some with sister Elizabeth)
Larger format photographs on card stock (16; various poses, ages)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Husband of Adele Dwight Garrison.

did.unittitle: 2 portraits, 1 taken photographer Frederick Gutekunst when Garrison was a student at the University of Pennsylvania and the other at an older age

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Son of Adele Dwight and F. Lynwood Garrison.

did.unittitle: Portraits of Garrison as a child, including by photographer F. Gutekunst (8, some duplicates)

Later portraits, including in military uniform

did.unittitle: 3 portraits; 9 informal photographs with siblings, father

3 cartes de visite, 1 larger format portrait of three children
Marie Rose Reine Laure Bonhote Lehmann

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mother of Laure (Laura) Emma Lehmann Dwight who was the wife of Henry E. Dwight

did.unittitle: Adele Emma Lehmann Tack and family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sister of Laura Lehmann Dwight.

did.unittitle: 12 portraits of Adele, her husband Augustus Tack and their children Adele and Theodore (some duplicates)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sister of Laura Lehmann Dwight

did.unittitle: 9 portraits of Laure, her husband Charles Witthoff and their children Charles and Annie
Elizabeth Billingsley; Agnes Bowen; Henry Bryant; Josephine Carr; Grenville Cole; Marcellus Dodge; Lawrence Henderson
T. A. Rickard; Kathleen and William Smyth; Georgie Snow
Interior shots of Loockerman mansion, Dover, Delaware
Photo of lace piece lent to a museum by Elizabeth Bradford Dwight
Photos of Sawyer House, Andover, MA, mountains, church in mountain setting
Carte de visite album including various members of the Dwight and Bradford families
Small album of photos taken in New Haven at the time of the Yale Commencement, including homes of Dwight family members
Brief from Jonathan Belcher, Governor of the Province of Massachusetts Bay, soliciting contributions for Charlestown, South Carolina, following fire
"To the Inhabitants of America" - broadside from Benedict Arnold, printed in New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In this broadside, New Haven native and former Continental Army leader Benedict Arnold explains why he has decided "to join the King's arms," regarding the revolutionary leaders as "Usurpers" and "criminals" who are serving their own interests.

did.unittitle: Poem commemorating the death of George Washington, printed in Hartford
The Free Press - 4 page publication "Published in the Interest of Pennsylvania Week, Philadelphia, October 13-19, 1947 by John Wanamaker"
Two issues of The Eastern Herald and Maine Gazette
Supplement to Public Ledger Philadelphia - tribute commemorating the death of General Ulysses S. Grant
Issue of The Smyrna Times , Smyrna, Delaware
Issue of The Andover Townsman , Andover, Massachusetts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including regarding John Adams, fashion show in Dover Delaware, rare books found in safe, and "A Brief Sketch of the History of Orthoepy"

did.unittitle: Devout Exercises of the Heart, / by the late pious & ingenious Mrs. Elizabeth Rowe. ; Abridged for the use of the pious (New-York: : Printed by Tiebout & O'Brien for E. Mitchell, book-seller no. 9 Maiden-Lane)
Two travel books: Views From Bible Lands - Asia Minor and the Islands of the Levant , The Sights of London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Views From Bible Lands is inscribed with the name of Laure Emma Lehmann.

did.unittitle: Old Grimes by Albert G. Greene ; illustrated by Augustus Hoppin (Providence : S.S. Rider & Brother)
Yale Alumni Association of Fairfield County, Connecticut Record of the First Reunion, Greenfield Hill
Glimpses of Andover, Massachusetts , (John N. Cole, Publisher, Andover)
Some of the Ancestors of Rodman Stoddard of Woodbury, Conn. and Detroit, Mich. compiled by Edward Deacon (Bridgeport, 1893)
A Catalogue of autograph letters and documents relating to the Declaration of Independence and the Revolutionary War... (Philadelphia, The Rosenbach Company)
Prospectus for Young Ladies' Seminary for Boarding and Day Pupils, Philadelphia
Bond for British prisoner of war, Joseph Willson [or Wilson]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This printed bond with blanks filled in with handwritten information was apparently given to a paroled prisoner, possibly to give him access to work on a farm or in some other capacity in the vicinity of the place where the prisoners were being held.

did.unittitle: Printed inscription from Lord Nelson monument
Documentation related to the gift of Mrs. F. L. Garrison to Yale University of a circular and letter by Benjamin Silliman
Bradshaw's Map of Parts of France, Switzerland, and the Rhine

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes autographs of Henry Clay, Timothy Dwight, Benjamin Franklin, Daniel Webster, William H. Seward and others.

did.unittitle: Notebook with notes and autographs in German
Scroll with handwritten record of the kings of France
Leather wallet containing newspaper clippings re. death of Henry E. Dwight, lock of hair, calling card of H. E. Dwight
Lock of hair of William Dwight Garrison, deceased infant son of Adele Dwight Garrison with letter from M. W. D.
Textile with embroidery and lace edge
Floral design on waxed paper with motto ""Dreams And Rosy Slumbers and Light / A fair Good Night to Each and All"
Wax seals (3), with family coats of arms(?)
Small bas relief sculpture of bust of J. Huss in wooden case
2 silver pendants: Peucinian Society of Bowdoin College and Alpha of Maine
